data.title: Stock market sees Dow surging, Nasdaq turning negative as small caps lead the way. Quantum stocks soar. Shopify jumps; AI stocks hit.
data.description: In today's stock market, small-cap companies are outperforming with several AI stocks, including Palantir, experiencing selling pressure while Shopify sees a significant increase. The Dow Jones Industrial Average is surging, but the Nasdaq Composite has turned negative. Quantum stocks are soaring amidst live coverage of the market events.

data.title: Tech labor market declining since early 2022, 150,000 tech workers laid off in 2024, job postings down 36% in mid-2025.
data.description: The tech labor market in the U.S. has been declining, with a hiring surge post-pandemic followed by cutbacks in 2022 and 2023. Despite a rebound in tech stocks fueled by AI investments, actual employment numbers did not improve. AI companies are spending billions with little return, leading to mass layoffs with at least 150,000 tech workers laid off in 2024 alone. Tech job postings dropped by 36% below pre-pandemic levels by mid-2025, with a continued downward trend expected. In August, only 73,000 jobs were added to the market, with significant downward revisions. President Trump ousted the head of the Bureau of Labor Statistics due to alleged data distortion. Full story available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: HYROX partners with Atmosphere TV to stream all 2025 and 2026 events in over 60,000 venues worldwide.
data.description: A fitness racing event, HYROX, has teamed up with Atmosphere TV to stream all HYROX Majors and Regional Championships for the 2025 and 2026 seasons. This partnership aims to bring HYROX competitions to more than 60,000 establishments globally, including bars, restaurants, gyms, and retail venues. This collaboration is a significant step as it marks the first time HYROX events will be streamed at a global level, increasing its reach to a wider audience.

data.title: Omega Healthcare Investors, Inc. (NYSE:OHI) to release Q3 2025 earnings on Oct 30 after market close, conference call on Oct 31.
data.description: Headquartered in Hunt Valley, Maryland, Omega Healthcare Investors, Inc. (NYSE: OHI) disclosed plans to unveil its financial results for the quarter concluding on September 30, 2025, post market hours on Thursday, October 30, 2025. Subsequently, on Friday, October 31, 2025, at 10 a.m. Eastern Time, the company will host a conference call to discuss the results for the third quarter of 2025 and provide updates on ongoing developments. The call will be available to investors and interested parties for participation.

data.description: Since its launch earlier this year, Coinbase's on-chain lending product has facilitated loans totaling more than $1 billion.

data.title: WSS pays tribute to everyday heroes with "We Know What It Takes" campaign, showcasing stories and cultural collections. Los Angeles, Oct. 3, 2025.
data.description: A retailer in the neighborhood of Los Angeles is paying tribute to the resilience of everyday heroes by sharing stories, showcasing cultural collections, and hosting community events. On October 3, 2025, WSS, a retailer known for its focus on sneakers, affordability, and community engagement, announced the launch of its new initiative titled "We Know What It Takes." This program aims to celebrate individuals who demonstrate strength and courage in their daily lives.

data.title: PG&E protects thousands with 1,000 miles of energized underground powerlines, reduces system-wide risk by 8.4%.
data.description: In a recent development, Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E) has successfully installed and activated 1,000 miles of underground powerlines in 27 counties across California. This initiative comes as part of a broader effort to mitigate the risk of wildfires, particularly in high-risk areas. By implementing these underground powerlines, the system-wide risk has been reduced by 8.4%, showcasing the company's commitment to enhancing safety measures. The completion of this project marks a significant milestone in PG&E's ongoing efforts to bolster the resilience of its infrastructure and protect communities from potential fire hazards.

data.title: DXP Enterprises, Inc. acquired APSCO, LLC, a manufacturer's rep of pumps and equipment, serving 8 states. Acquisition funded with cash.
data.description: In Houston, DXP Enterprises, Inc. (NASDAQ: DXPE) has successfully finalized the purchase of APSCO, LLC, a company established in 1986 and based in Redmond, Washington. Operating in Alaska, Colorado, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Oregon, Washington, Wyoming, and nearby regions, APSCO stands as a prominent manufacturer's representative specializing in pumps, controls, and process equipment for the water and wastewater sector. The acquisition was financed by DXP using cash funds.

data.title: CarInsurance.com report shows major car insurance rate disparities among U.S. cities due to various factors.
data.description: A report by CarInsurance.com analyzed car insurance premiums in various U.S. cities, showcasing significant cost variations among neighboring areas and states. The study titled "The Most Expensive Cities for Car Insurance in 2025 (and the Cheapest)" emphasized the impact of factors like population density, traffic hazards, crime rates, weather conditions, and local regulations on insurance rates. The analysis revealed noteworthy disparities in premium costs, underscoring the influence of diverse factors on insurance pricing.

data.title: CTEK emphasizes the importance of regular car battery charging on Oct. 5, 2025. Never stop charging. #ChargeYourCarDay
data.description: On Sunday, October 5th, the city of Chicago will observe Charge Your Car Day. CTEK, a leading provider of smart battery charging solutions, emphasizes the importance of regular battery care for North American drivers. Avoiding a once-a-year approach, CTEK encourages frequent maintenance to ensure optimal performance.
